rules.description: Deploying Azure Load Balancer backend pools with at least two instances prevents a single point of failure. Pairing with Virtual Machine Scale Sets can provide additional scalability.
rules.query: mv-expand bpool = properties.backendAddressPools | extend BackendAddresses = array_length(bpool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses) | where BackendAddresses <= 1
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: To avoid service disruptions, migrate to Inbound NAT rule V2.
rules.query: where sku.name =~ 'Standard' | extend inboundNATRULES=todynamic(properties['inboundNatRules']) | mv-expand inboundNATRULES | where isnotempty(inboundNATRULES.properties.backendIPConfiguration) and array_length(properties.inboundNatPools) == 0
rules.fix: To avoid service disruptions, migrate to Inbound NAT rule V2.

rules.description: To keep the workloads appropriately distributed, you need to upgrade to Standard Load Balancer.
rules.fix: To keep the workloads appropriately distributed, you need to upgrade to Standard Load Balancer.

rules.description: Outbound rules for Standard Public Load Balancer involve manual port allocation for backend pools, limiting scalability and risk of SNAT port exhaustion. NAT Gateway is recommended for its dynamic scaling and secure internet connectivity.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation
